 Thanks for the kind words.

The other thing is that lovers of classic literature will usually find more info on their own out of a deep and abiding interest about writers and works than could ever be made available here. So who are we writing these literary articles for? A true reader will search things out like an Antiquary, and how many of those use Wikipedia as a reference? When it comes to writing articles on individual works, I favor reading the works themselves, rather than some trifle of an article, even if it's ``scholarly``. I won't even start on the strange pseudo-government and society of Wikipedia, the invitation into which I must've misplaced.  
  
 I used to read 5+ hours per day, and I miss it too much.
